title: Keep the LiveView process snappy
description: A LiveView is a process per connection, so blocking it freezes that user's UI; offload slow work and stream large data.
body: Use when building Phoenix LiveView event handlers or mounts. Do not run slow queries or external calls synchronously inside handle_event/mount, since each blocks that connection's process and its UI. Actions: do heavy work in a Task and send the result back to the LiveView to assign, use temporary assigns / streams for large collections to avoid holding everything in socket state, and keep mount fast (use connected?/2 to defer work to the connected mount). Validation signals include a Phoenix.LiveViewTest exercising mount and events without the process stalling. Risk boundary: a blocked LiveView process makes one user's page unresponsive and, repeated across users, exhausts schedulers; never put secrets in socket assigns sent to the client.
